---
title: "NYC"
format: docx
editor: visual
---

## Libraries

```{r}

#install.packages('tidyverse')
  library(tidyverse)
#install.packages('dplyr')
  library(dplyr)
#install.packages('gmodels')
  library(gmodels)
#install.packages('readxl')
  library(readxl)
#install.packages('lubridate')
  library(lubridate)
#install.packages('tableone')
  library(tableone)
#install.packages('knitr')
  library(knitr)

```

## Dataset 

Mutating Date Column

```{r}

data2 <- read.csv("previous_years.csv")

data1 <- read.csv("edheat2025_live.csv")

data <- bind_rows(data2, data1)
#Combines data sets with similar rows and columns

data$Date <- as.Date(data$END_DATE)
#Turns end date column into date

data$END_DATE <- NULL

```

## Create Temperature Ranges

```{r}

data$TEMP_RANGE <- cut(data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP,
                       
                       breaks = c(-Inf, 60, 80, 90, Inf),
                       
                       labels = c("Cold", "Warm", "Hot", "Extreme Heat")
)

```

## Descriptive Statistics

```{r}

# subset(data, as.integer(format(Date, "%Y")) == 2018)

for (i in 2017:2025) {
  print(summary(subset(data, as.integer(format(Date, "%Y")) == i)))
}

ds_table <- CreateTableOne(data = data)
kable(print(ds_table, showAllLevels = TRUE, printToggle = FALSE, noSpaces = TRUE))

print(ds_table)

```

## Figures just to see

```{r}


plot(data$Date, data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP, 
     main = "Max Daily Temp vs. Time",  
     xlab = "Date",     
     ylab = "Max Daily Temp",        
     col = "blue",                
     pch = 19,                     
     type = "b")


 
```

```{r}
doy <- as.numeric(format(data$Date, "%j"))

plot(doy[idx], data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP[idx],
     type = "l",
     col = "blue",
     xlab = "Day of Year",
     ylab = "Max Daily Temp",
     main = "Max Daily Temperature: 2018 vs 2019",
     ylim = range(data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP[c(idx, idx2)], na.rm = TRUE))

lines(doy[idx2], data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP[idx2],
      col = "green")

legend("topright",
       legend = c("2018", "2019"),
       col = c("blue", "green"),
       lty = 1)
```

```{r}
idx <- format(data$Date, "%Y") == "2017"
idx2 <- format(data$Date, "%Y") == "2025"

# Create dates with the same year (2000)
date2018 <- as.Date(paste0("2000-", format(data$Date[idx], "%m-%d")))
date2025 <- as.Date(paste0("2000-", format(data$Date[idx2], "%m-%d")))

plot(date2018, data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP[idx],
     type = "l",
     col = "blue",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Max Daily Temp",
     main = "Max Daily Temperature: 2017 vs 2025",
     ylim = range(data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP[c(idx, idx2)], na.rm = TRUE))

lines(date2025, data$MAX_DAILY_TEMP[idx2],
      col = "green")

legend("topright",
       legend = c("2017", "2025"),
       col = c("blue", "green"),
       lty = 1)
```

```{r}
# install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2) 
# Create a sample categorical vector

# Calculate the frequency table
range_counts <- table(data$TEMP_RANGE)

# Create the bar plot
barplot(range_counts, 
        main = "Frequency of Temp Ranges", 
        xlab = "Temp Range in Degrees F", 
        ylab = "Count", 
        names.arg = c("Below 60", "60-79", "80-89", "Equal or above 90"),
        col = c("blue", "lightblue", "orange", "red"))
```
